title: Sour Irish Lamb Stew
description: A hearty, tangy Irish lamb stew thats dense and satisfying, perfect for a cozy datenight meal.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, bone‑in, cut into 2‑inch pieces

instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium‑high heat.
Add the lamb pieces and brown them on all sides, about 5–6 minutes.
Transfer the browned lamb to a plate and set aside.
In the same pot, sauté the onion and garlic until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the carrots, parsnips, and potatoes, stirring to coat with oil.
Stir in the chicken stock concentrate and 3 cups of water, bringing the mixture to a gentle boil.
Add the lemon juice, bay leaf, thyme, salt, and pepper, then return the lamb to the pot.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 90 minutes, or until the lamb is tender.
Remove the bay leaf, taste, and adjust seasonings if necessary.
Serve the stew hot, ladled into bowls with a side of crusty sourdough if desired.
Enjoy the rich, tangy flavors of this classic Irish lamb stew.
